item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a suggestion: what about a test where they play the SCP that was a song that people wrote with their own blood (SCP-012 I think? Definitely around that number)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, I'll get on editing that soon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's more the combination of small errors that demonstrates that you don't really know much about music. Stuff like:

+ Examples
- Examples

soft nameless melody with only the C and A cords

Which doesn't tell you the actual chords used, just the root note, and also your misspelling of 'chords'. Also, you misuse the word 'melody' here, as melody would be a sequence of single notes, not chords.

fast nameless melody with the E major, D minor, C major, and E.

Which has the 'melody' misuse, as well as having E in there twice.

a melody she titled 'Stuck.'

Again with misuse of melody.

Basically, my advice here would be to either read up on basic music theory/music terminology (youtube musicians are a great way to do this), or to simplify your descriptions such that you don't need to be versed in music to understand what is happening here, like you did with the deck the halls entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No one is obligated to give you more than their passing thoughts on your work if it's already on the mainlist. I would elaborate on my confusion, but looking through the rest of the comments here it seems like the ending, particularly what's going on with Agent Mollone, wasn't meant to be fully understood, so I don't think it would help you all that much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ya think you could be more specific? That sounds more like something you just say to yourself as you pass it by. If you have no real advice, please don't leave a comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This does absolutely nothing for me. It didn't make me feel anything, there's no one aspect I particularly like, and the only feeling I had after it was over was one of mild confusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried to do some research for it but I didn’t exactly get anywhere. Could you maybe suggest an sources to get it right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dr.Laytz played a fast nameless melody with the E major, D minor, C major, and E.

I think the lack of proper musical terminology in these logs really undercuts the impact. As a guitar player, it takes me out of it for sure, as it also probably does for any users who are versed in music theory. This one is a novote for me.

E: After rereading, I've noticed that there really is no clear escalation in the logs here, which kills the impact of the ending because it wasn't paced properly. Pushes me to a downvote.

item.title: Re: Novote
item.content:encoded:
The inconsistencies are a few things:

1) They’re different people and their memories of the event they witnessed aren’t all going to correlate because that’s basic psychology.

2) It’s just the mental affect. It actually technically affected none of them permanently. That’s just how each of their brains reacted to the trauma they witnessed.

And yeah she told me, that is pretty funny.

For Mollone you’re not exactly supposed to get it, but I suppose I should make that more clear. Thank you for your feedback and advice.

item.title: Novote
item.content:encoded:
The article is absolutely fine but it just doesn't work for me. I don't understand what's up with the Agent Mollone, I don't like the inconsistencies in the witness reports - it seems like the first two people were permanently affected by the SCP while the second two experienced a passing effect.

Also, it is a personal thing, but I don't like ellipses without spaces, but whatever.

For whatever reason Wikidot shows that Mew voted twice on the article and I find this glitch funny.

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
Credit for image

Here I am posting an article I've had sitting in my drafts forever.
